title.value: Highly Stereoselective Synthesis of Chiral Alkylallenes by Organocopper(I)-Induced Anti 1,3-Substitution of Chiral Propynyl Esters

abstract.value: The synthesis of chiral 1,3-dialkylallenes R1CH=C=CHR2 of high enantiomeric purity, by applying homogeneous reactions between organocopper(I) reagents of the type [{R2CuX}MgX·LiX] and chiral propynyl methanesulfonates or sulfinates at low temperatures in THF, is reported. The reactions are generally fast; typically complete conversion is obtained within a few minutes at -60 °C. Overall, high anti stereoselectivity is observed. A plausible mechanism is put forward, and comparison is made with the stereochemistry of reactions of comparable substrates with d10 complexes, e.g. of Pd(0).

warnings: Front-page text before the title appears to belong to a preceding article.
Initial rule-based author extraction included affiliations; corrected to named authors only.
Provided markdown/front blocks begin with unrelated text from a preceding article before the actual title page.
Product evidence also supports alkylallenes and 1,3-disubstituted allenes; the stereochemically explicit label was selected.
Leaving-group evidence includes both methanesulfonate/sulfonate and sulfinate substrates, but the schema permits only one leaving-group label.
